task:
Rewrite the seeded tech-stack local default at `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/memory/tech-stack.md` so it captures the founder-ratified CROSS-REPO BEST-OF default (CoCoder + CoBuilder), structured as a LAYERED stack: one shared toolchain plus per-surface UI profiles. Do NOT keep the current CoCoder-only first pass.

WRITE SCOPE: only `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/memory/tech-stack.md`. Touch nothing else.

WHAT TO PRODUCE (sections):
1. Shared toolchain (applies to every new repo): pnpm, TypeScript (strict + the extra strict flags noUncheckedIndexedAccess / noImplicitOverride, target ES2022), Node baseline, Vitest, Playwright, Turbo, knip, and a MINIMAL ESLint 9 flat config (restrained — a few rules, e.g. security; NOT a large ruleset). Note that CoCoder has no linter today and that the engine repo's own ESLint adoption is tracked separately as ticket 0048 (out of scope here).
2. Desktop profile: electron-vite / Vite 5 / React 18 / Electron.
3. Web profile: Next.js 15 / React 19 / Tailwind v4 / shadcn.
4. Services profile (optional): Fastify / tRPC.

VERSION PINNING (mandatory, no invented versions): pin EVERY version from the LIVE config of whichever repo the choice came from. Read the actual files — for CoCoder this repo's root `package.json`, `packages/*/package.json`, `tsconfig*.json`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`; for CoBuilder read `/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoBuilder/infrastructure` root `package.json`, app/package manifests, `tsconfig*`, and lockfile. State, per pinned tool, which repo's live config it came from. If a tool exists in both repos at different versions, prefer the newer and say so. Give a ONE-LINE rationale per choice.

KEEP: the workspace-wins resolution note at the end (workspace that specifies its own value wins; otherwise this local default applies; seeded create-only into each scaffolded repo's cocoder/memory/ and never overwritten).

ACCEPTANCE: file reflects the layered cross-repo best-of; every version pin is traceable to a live config file you actually read (cite which repo); ESLint 9 minimal flat config is present as the lint default; resolution note retained. MUST NOT BREAK: the scaffold/mutations test file-set pins — run `pnpm test` (or the scaffold/mutations suite) and confirm green before you report done. Report the exact versions you pinned with their source repo, and the test command + result as evidence.

Rewrite the design-spec local-default OWNER at `packages/ui/src/renderer/styles/design-spec.md` so it is a SELF-CONTAINED, DATED snapshot of the CoBuilder "Fusion" design system (used across both the IDE and the website), replacing the current CoCoder-dashboard-only catalog. This is the founder-ratified cross-repo design default.

WRITE SCOPE: only `packages/ui/src/renderer/styles/design-spec.md`. Also permitted IF its description no longer matches: `templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/memory/design-spec.md` (the template pointer) — update only its prose so it still correctly points at this owner; do NOT copy token tables into it. Touch nothing else.

UPSTREAM SOURCE (read these live files): `/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoBuilder/infrastructure/design-system` — token SSOT files `design/tokens-website.css` (web `--cb-*` hex/rgba) and `design/tokens-ide.css` (IDE shadcn-HSL); live web values in `/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoBuilder/infrastructure/cobuilder-website/app/globals.css`. Also read `design-system/README.md`/`AGENTS.md` for the design language framing (Art Deco x Liquid Glass; Warm Espresso dark / Warm Linen light; fonts Josefin Sans / Inter / JetBrains Mono; 4px spacing grid; low radius; glass 22px blur + inset highlight; motion 150/250/400ms).

WHAT TO PRODUCE — a single owner document containing:
1. Header naming `CoBuilder/infrastructure/design-system` as the upstream SSOT and a SNAPSHOT DATE of 2026-06-24, with a one-line note that re-sync is mechanical against that SSOT (founder accepted the drift trade-off vs a live cross-repo reference).
2. The resolution rule (workspace that defines its own CSS/design wins; otherwise this local default applies; seeded create-only and never overwritten).
3. BOTH token surfaces as snapshotted tables: the web `--cb-*` hex/rgba tokens (dark + light) AND the IDE shadcn-HSL tokens. Copy actual values from the live files.
4. Typography (the three font families + type/letter-spacing scale), spacing (4px grid), radius ladder, glass surface recipe, and motion tokens.
5. Core component patterns (glass shell/panels, gold-line accents, status chips, forms/buttons) — patterns, not a re-paste of CSS rules.

DRIFT RESOLUTION (mandatory): the light accent differs — token SSOT `design/tokens-website.css` has `--cb-accent: #8B7545` while live `cobuilder-website/app/globals.css` has `#7A663B`. Snapshot the LIVE `globals.css` value (`#7A663B`) and add an explicit note recording the chosen value and that it diverges from the token SSOT. Apply the same live-globals-wins rule to any other web `--cb-*` value that differs between the two files, noting each divergence.

ACCEPTANCE: design-spec.md is self-contained (a fresh reader needs no access to the CoBuilder repo to apply it), dated 2026-06-24, names the SSOT, covers BOTH token surfaces with real copied values, resolves the accent drift to the live value with a recorded note, remains the SINGLE owner (template pointer not forked into a second catalog). MUST NOT BREAK: the scaffold/mutations test file-set pins — run `pnpm test` and confirm green before reporting done. Report the files changed, the accent value you snapshotted (and any other divergences found), and the test command + result as evidence.
